name: Generate invoices with HTTP / REST API and Notion automatically

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf03333-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Download Source File

### Download Source File

GET ={{ $json.fileUrl }} — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Parse CSV Contents

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Parse CSV Contents

Parses the incoming CSV binary into JSON items.

Next: Clean & Reshape Rows

### Clean & Reshape Rows

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming document records to a stable schema

Next: Load Rows into Notion

### Load Rows into Notion

notion — `create` operation against the connected account.

Next: Align Field Names

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

# Generate invoices with HTTP / REST API and Notion automatically

**Pattern:** Document Generation · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Generate invoices with HTTP / REST API and Notion automatically. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual summarization is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Download Source File → Parse CSV Contents → Clean & Reshape Rows → Load Rows into Notion → Align Field Names → Cap Batch Volume → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to document generation without manual effort
- Anyone automating summarization in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Improves response time
- Standardizes the process every time
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Notion integration token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your summarization process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
